facts: Cells are the basic building blocks of all living things; a single-celled organism like an amoeba is a complete living thing made of just one cell, while a human is made of trillions of cells.
Mitosis is a type of cell division that produces two identical daughter cells, enabling an organism to grow and repair damaged tissue such as skin after a cut.
Tissues are groups of similar cells that work together to perform a specific function; for example, muscle tissue is made of muscle cells that contract to move parts of the body.
Organs are structures made of two or more different types of tissues working together; the stomach contains muscle tissue for churning food and epithelial tissue for lining its inner surface.

An organ system is a collection of organs that work together to carry out a major life process; the digestive system includes the stomach, intestines, and liver to break down food and absorb nutrients.
All new cells arise from existing cells through division; when a bone breaks, cells in the bone divide (mitosis) to repair the fracture.
misconceptions: Cells exist only in animals and humans; plants are not made of cells.
Growth occurs because individual cells swell up like balloons, not because cells divide to form more cells.
An organ like the heart is made entirely of one kind of tissue, such as only muscle tissue.
